item.title: The best deals on MacBooks right now
item.description:
Source: The Verge
It’s 2026, and that means Apple sells MacBooks equipped with not just its capable M-series processors but A-series chips that were previously exclusive to the iPhone. With the MacBook Neo starting at $599, the company now offers a wider range of options with more sizes and price points than ever before, all of which run […]
Matched: budget(+3), war(+3), election(+2), ai(+3), launch(+2), who(+2)

item.title: Grammarly is using our identities without permission
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Grammarly's "expert review" feature offers to give users writing advice "inspired by" subject matter experts, including recently deceased professors, as Wired reported on Wednesday. When I tried the feature out myself, I found some experts that came as a surprise for a different reason - one of them was my boss. The AI-generated feedback included […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), launch(+2), who(+2)

item.title: Nintendo is suing the US government for a refund of Trump’s illegal tariffs
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Nintendo of America is suing the US government over President Trump's tariffs and is demanding a "prompt refund, with interest" of any duties that it has paid, according to a complaint filed in the US Court of International Trade, as reported previously by Aftermath. The Supreme Court ruled last month that Trump's use of the […]
Matched: law(+3), court(+2), ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: The best microSD Express card for the Switch 2 is the cheapest one you can find
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Unlike the original Switch, the Nintendo Switch 2 requires microSD Express cards for storing and playing games. While physically identical to microSD cards (aside from a small, easy-to-miss “EX” emblem etched onto the front of the card, as highlighted in the main image above), microSD Express cards are significantly faster. They advertise a 4.4x increase […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: OSHA probing fatality at Rivian warehouse
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
A 61-year-old worker died on Thursday after reportedly getting stuck between a tractor trailer and a loading dock.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: You can already save up to $50 on the new M4 iPad Air
item.description:
Source: The Verge
In case you missed it, Apple announced a host of new devices this week, including both a new phone and a cheaper laptop. It also unveiled the M4-powered iPad Air, which is already receiving a preorder discount ahead of its March 11th debut. Right now, the 128GB / Wi-Fi model is available at Amazon, Best […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: The Trump phone was a no-show at the world’s biggest mobile show
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This time, we tried, and failed, to find it at the world's biggest mobile trade show. This week Barcelona was taken over by the tech industry as Mobile World Congress […]
Matched: ai(+3), launch(+2), who(+2)

item.title: The Trump administration says it can’t process tariff refunds because of computer problems
item.description:
Source: The Verge
The US Customs and Border Protection says it currently can't comply with an order to process billions of dollars in refunds stemming from tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. In a filing on Friday, CBP executive director Brandon Lord says the agency's digital import processing system is "not well suited to a task of this […]
Matched: policy(+3), court(+2)

item.title: The AI Doc is an overwrought hype piece for doomers and accelerationists alike
item.description:
Source: The Verge
We are in the thick of a massive push to incorporate generative AI into almost every aspect of our lives, but it is still easy to be confused about what it is and how it works. It doesn't help that many of gen AI's proponents and detractors both speak about it with a feverish hyperbole […]
Matched: ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: TriZetto confirms 3.4M people’s health and personal data was stolen during breach
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Health tech giant TriZetto has confirmed that more than 3.4 million people had personal and health information stolen in a 2024 cyberattack, which the company failed to detect for almost a year.
Matched: ai(+3), breach(+3)

item.title: Indie games are turning the act of looking into an art
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Adriaan de Jongh and Sylvain Tegroeg did not necessarily set out to create a new genre. But, in some ways, that's exactly what the duo did when they released Hidden Folks in 2017. Hidden object games have been around for decades - they've existed well before computers, when people searched for objects in paintings and […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.
Matched: court(+2), ai(+3)

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.
Matched: ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Trump’s surgeon general nominee is running the wellness grifter playbook perfectly
item.description:
Source: The Verge
This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. We'll be taking a break next week and will back March 20th. Opt in for Optimizer here. On the surface, the wellness to […]
Matched: vaccine(+3)

item.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Quantum scale-up Pasqal plans $2B SPAC listing, promises to ‘remain French’
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
A SPAC deal that is set to take French quantum company Pasqal public on the Nasdaq values the company at $2 billion pre-money.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: India’s Karnataka signals intent to ban social media for under-16s
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Karnataka's proposal highlights a growing global debate over children’s rights and online policy.
Matched: policy(+3)

item.title: After Europe, WhatsApp will let rival AI companies offer chatbots in Brazil
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Meta is now allowing rival AI companies to provide their chatbots on WhatsApp to Brazilian users for a fee, a day after the company confirmed a similar decision for users in Europe.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Bill Gates’ TerraPower gets approval to build new nuclear reactor
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
It's the first permit to be issued by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission in nearly a decade.
